programme.desc: Canada's prairies are among the world's most endangered, least protected ecosystems, but there are people working to keep them wild.

programme.title: Mr. Dressup: The Magic of Make-Believe
programme.desc: A detailed look at a famed, iconic television show from Canada that impacted multiple generations and its ongoing legacy.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: Investigating the unsolved 2015 murder of John Palmer, who was nicknamed Goldfinger after his involvement in melting down gold from the 1983 Brink's-Mat robbery. Palmer was found dead in his Essex garden and while police originally believed he died of natural causes, they eventually confirmed the case was being treated as a professional hit.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: The search for John Palmer's killer leads to Tenerife, where he built a timeshare empire. Undercover stings and Russian mob ties expose the scale of his power.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: A professional hitman is suspected in John Palmer's murder, with evidence pointing to a cartel and an Eastern European enforcer.

programme.title: Discovering: Alan Rickman
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, who starred in Die Hard, Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves and Love Actually, as well as the Harry Potter franchise.

programme.title: The Seventies
programme.desc: A look at how the dramatic shifting of gender roles and the relaxing of censorship created new artistic and social directions in America.

programme.title: Discovering: Harrison Ford
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, whose roles in the Star Wars and Indiana Jones franchises have made him one of the biggest stars in the world.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of director Ridley Scott, whose films include Alien, Blade Runner, Thelma & Louise, Gladiator, Prometheus, The Martian and Hannibal.

programme.title: The Movies
programme.desc: The journey through American cinema continues into the 1980s with hits that became cult classics, from ET to The Breakfast Club.

programme.title: Janet Jackson
programme.desc: A profile of the singer in which she speaks candidly about her early years, her career and the defining moments of her life. At the age of seven, Janet's father Joe Jackson put her to work in the family show. Her father never asked if she wanted to be a performer, so it was down to Janet to find her own path.

programme.title: Escobar by Escobar
programme.desc: The story behind the cursed Escobar empire, following the fortunes of Juan Pablo, who was the sole heir to the biggest drug dealer in history.

programme.title: Bruno v Tyson
programme.desc: The story of the boxing rivalry between Frank Bruno and Mike Tyson, spanning two epic fights marred by politics and volatile race relations, with the old foes coming together for a reunion.

programme.title: My Icon: Duke McKenzie
programme.desc: The former world champion boxer talks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: Bobby
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of director Ridley Scott, whose films include Alien, Blade Runner, Thelma & Louise, Gladiator, Prometheus, The Martian and Hannibal.

programme.title: Discovering: Harrison Ford
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, whose roles in the Star Wars and Indiana Jones franchises have made him one of the biggest stars in the world.

programme.title: Dart Kings
programme.desc: A look at the game during the 1970s, '80s and '90s, shining a spotlight on unlikely sporting heroes including Eric Bristow, Jocky Wilson and Phil Taylor.

programme.title: I Am Jackie O
programme.desc: Profile of Jackie Kennedy Onassis, providing a definitive exploration of her life, from first lady to widow, unofficial royalty and fashion icon.

programme.title: My Icon: Denise Lewis
programme.desc: The former British heptathlete and Olympic gold medallist speaks about the figures who have been an inspiration over the course of her life.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: Documentary about the boy band, featuring rare and exclusive interviews with the members, who talk candidly about how they grappled with the true cost of global fame.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's growing success with their first number one single. But as their popularity soars, so does the tabloid mania.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's split which came to an end when Mikey proposed a reunion. The comeback proved a huge hit - but then tragedy struck and the tabloids descended.

programme.title: Gascoigne
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gar

programme.title: Wild Rockies
programme.desc: Getting up close and personal with the animals that live in the Rocky Mountains. The predator-prey relationship is a delicate balance in the Rockies, where elk and moose live alongside bears and wolves.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the work of a rescue centre founded by Hope Swinimer as it rehabilitates more than 1,000 animals every year.

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Examining the Amazon rainforest's oxygen-breathing trees and following flamingos that thrive in the sun's scorching UV rays.

programme.title: Superbeast
programme.desc: A celebration of nature's ultimate athletes as they survive, excel and dominate in Earth's toughest arenas, from apex predators to cunning creatures. Gorillas smash termite mounds to pieces, jaguars crack the skulls of caimans with apparent ease, and orcas hunt their prey as a team.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: A new term starts at the school in Borneo, as newbie Bumi struggles to focus and star student Valentino experiences a bumpy start to college.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: An exploration of the Mesoamerican Reef, 40 miles north of Honduras - a pristine home for an abundance of amazing marine wildlife and lush vegetation.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Hope helps the most Canadian wildlife rescue ever reach a happy ending when a beaver is saved, while the rehab is flooded with orphaned seals frozen solid in ice.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Oshine is introduced to the youngest members of the nursery, Lingga and Dinda, and animal director Jeremy hopes the encounter will be more successful than the orangutan's disastrous meeting with foster mum A-mei. Meanwhile, with the boss otherwise occupied, there is a free-for-all at Hananya's when the chimps are given a large donation of fruit.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The male capuchins explore their new outdoor enclosure for the first time, Hananya shows everyone who is boss at feeding time, and baby woolly monkey Diego plucks up the courage to join his family. Meanwhile, the keepers are overjoyed to discover golden-cheeked gibbon Zoey is pregnant again.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School

programme.desc: The habitats of Cambodia, which lies almost entirely within the Mekong basin, flanked by Thailand, Laos and Vietnam.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders

programme.desc: NFL players reach out to Chip and Joanna to help renovate the home for a veteran to make it more accessible for him.

programme.desc: A dad who's renovated every home he's ever owned puts in the work to get a waterfront property in Ocean City, Md.; he gets help from his daughter and her family to make his and his wife's dream vacation house a reality.
